topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The worn leather of the ledger felt suddenly cold beneath my fingertips. It wasn't the paper itself, but the numbers scrawled across the page—a collection of figures that made my stomach clench. I’d been tidying the attic, a task I usually found soothing, but today the discovery had sent a tremor through my hands. Tucked behind a stack of old holiday photos was a small, unassuming bank statement. My breath hitched. The account number was familiar, belonging to Liam. A wave of unexpected disorientation washed over me, leaving a hollow ache in my chest. I traced the figures, each digit a tiny, sharp intrusion into the quiet space we’d built. I felt a sudden urge to retreat, to bury myself under the dusty sheets, afraid that if I looked too closely, the fragile structure of our shared life would simply crumble.

[story 2]

"Are you alright?" Liam’s voice was light, casual, completely oblivious to the sudden stiffness in my posture. I stared at the opened folder on the kitchen counter, the glossy paper reflecting the harsh overhead light. Inside, the balance was staggering. My gaze darted from one entry to the next, the sheer volume of accumulated wealth creating a dizzying effect. A tightness formed behind my eyes, blurring the edges of the room. I swallowed hard, my throat suddenly dry. I didn't want to look at him; the reality of this secret felt too heavy, too exposed. When he finally approached, his smile faltered as he noticed the tension radiating off me. "What is it?" he asked, his tone shifting, becoming cautious. I could only shake my head, a small, involuntary movement, unable to articulate the vast, unsettling weight settling in my core.

[story 3]

I found the deposit slip hidden inside a seldom-used book. The ink seemed to vibrate under my scrutiny. It was proof—concrete evidence of a life lived parallel to the one we shared. My palms grew slick, and I had to grip the edge of the table to keep from shaking. Every line item felt like a tiny accusation. A profound sense of vulnerability washed over me, making my shoulders slump inward. I wanted to incinerate the paper, to erase the evidence of this hidden world, but my fingers remained frozen above it. I felt a deep, internal tremor, a kind of fragile imbalance, as I realized the depth of the unspoken reality that had been existing just beneath the surface of our everyday routine.
